data_quality_notes: MEGAN 2.1 has no direct URL; NCAR data server requires authentication. | World Bank has no direct biogenic VOC / isoprene indicator. | Substitution: MEGAN biogenic VOC data unavailable. Using World Bank 'Forest area (% of land area)' (AG.LND.FRST.ZS) as a proxy. Forests are the dominant source of biogenic VOCs (isoprene/monoterpenes); higher forest cover implies higher biogenic VOC emission potential. This is an acknowledged approximation — actual emissions also depend on temperature, LAI, and species composition.
summary: Used forest area (% land) as proxy for biogenic VOC emission potential (MEGAN data unavailable). Forests are the primary source of isoprene/monoterpenes. Pearson r=-0.495 (p=0.0000), Spearman rho=-0.489 (p=0.0000), n=3980 observations across 201 countries. Partial r=-0.524 (p=0.0000) controlling for log(GDP/capita). Best functional form: quadratic. Verdict: rejected.
